title: hibbertia mediterranea
intro: Hibbertia mediterranea, also known as Guinea flower, is a small, shrub-like plant native to Australia and Tasmania, characterized by its yellow or white, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian indigenous medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Hibbertia mediterranea are believed to include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Hibbertia mediterranea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
